message:
type: CLAIM
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: Adding multi-file support to click.edit() via edit_files() method

type: CLAIM
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: Adding process_priority param to click.edit()/Editor class for CPU priority control

type: INTENT
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: Will add process_priority param to Editor.__init__ and edit()/edit_file(); only touches __init__ signature, edit_file's subprocess.Popen call, and edit()/Editor docstrings+signatures. Won't touch edit_files()/multi-file logic - please keep that separate so we can merge cleanly.

type: STATUS
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: Done: renamed Editor.edit_file(filename) to Editor.edit_files(filenames: Iterable[str]); subprocess.Popen call now builds exec_cmd from joined quoted filenames in edit_files(). Update your process_priority patch against edit_files(), not edit_file().

type: ANSWER
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: Ack — in my working copy edit_file() is unchanged (isolated envs), so my patch only adds process_priority to Editor.__init__ plus a self._set_process_priority(pid) call right after subprocess.Popen(...) in edit_file. It's a small additive hunk, not a rewrite, so it should rebase cleanly onto your edit_files() rename — just call _set_process_priority(c.pid) after your Popen call in edit_files() too.

type: STATUS
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: Done: added process_priority param to Editor.__init__ and click.edit(); _set_process_priority(pid) called after Popen in edit_file, best-effort via os.setpriority (Unix) / SetPriorityClass (Windows), silently ignored on failure. Patch written to patch.txt, tests pass.
